channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 6 Moments of Anne Curtis’ Charming Presence on “It’s Showtime”
item.description: Anne Curtis continues to brighten up the Philippine noontime show scene with her signature ‘sosyal’ charm on ABS-CBN’s “It’s Showtime.” From her witty banter to playful interactions with co-hosts and ...

item.title: Edward surprises Katherine with a prom date in Episode 36 of The Alibi
item.description: In Episode 36 of the popular ABS-CBN series 'The Alibi,' Edward surprises Katherine with an unexpected prom date, adding a heartwarming moment to the story. The scene highlights Edward’s thoughtful an...

item.title: Madira: Rediscovering India’s Unique Cocktails with Traditional Ingredients
item.description: A recent book, 'Madira: India’s Forgotten Spirits and Cocktail Revival' by Parag A Shastry, brings together 101 cocktail recipes that creatively use uniquely Indian ingredients like jalebi, jackfruit ...

item.title: US-Based Kenyan Woman Holds Intimate Courthouse Wedding with Partner Abroad
item.description: Faith Chepkemoi, a Kenyan woman living in the United States, recently celebrated a key life milestone after getting married in a simple but meaningful ceremony abroad. The wedding took place at the De...

item.title: Arizona Sheriff Disputes FBI Director Kash Patel’s Claims Over Handling of Nancy Guthrie Disappearance
item.description: An Arizona sheriff has pushed back against accusations made by FBI Director Kash Patel regarding the investigation into the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, the 84-year-old mother of NBC journalist Sav...

item.title: DBE Defends Textbook Selection Process and Addresses Pricing Concerns

item.title: Toronto police charge teen in March synagogue shootings in Toronto and Vaughan
item.description: Toronto police have charged an 18-year-old male, who was 17 at the time of the incidents, in connection with two separate shootings at synagogues in Toronto and Vaughan that occurred on the night of M...

item.title: Nikita Tszyu secures sixth-round knockout amid disputed moments
item.description: Australian boxer Nikita Tszyu recorded a knockout victory over Diaz in the sixth round, continuing his rise in the professional ranks. The bout, however, was not without debate, as several moments dur...

item.title: Poll Shows Orange County Voters Disapprove of Trump’s Immigration Policies
item.description: A recent poll by UC Irvine’s School of Social Ecology indicates that support for President Trump in Orange County has dropped to 35%, with 65% of voters disapproving of his performance. The president’...

item.title: Smith College Faces Federal Review Over Transgender Student Admissions Policies
item.description: Smith College, a liberal arts women’s college in Massachusetts, is under federal investigation for potentially violating Title IX by admitting transgender students. Over the past decade, many U.S. wom...

item.title: Two-Year-Old Hearing Dog Enjoys Garden Party with King Charles III and Senior Royals
item.description: At the first garden party of the summer season at Buckingham Palace, a two-year-old cocker spaniel named Vegas, trained as a hearing dog for the deaf, captured the hearts of attendees and royals alike...

item.title: Southern Film Stars Who Entered Politics by Forming Their Own Parties
item.description: Several prominent film personalities from South India have successfully transitioned from cinema to politics by establishing their own political parties. This trend has been particularly notable in Ta...

item.title: Government Explains Closure of Changamwe Oil Refinery and Plans for Regional Refinery in Tanzania
item.description: The Kenyan government has clarified the reasons behind the closure of the Changamwe oil refinery in Mombasa, which stopped refining crude oil in September 2013 and now serves as a storage and distribu...

item.title: High Court confirms civil trial for Andrew Tate's sexual assault allegations will proceed once ready
item.description: Andrew Tate's civil trial concerning allegations of rape and sexual assault is set to proceed 'as soon as possible', a High Court judge has stated, despite recent delays. The trial, which was original...

item.title: Ara Mina Advocates Body Positivity and Shares Details About Upcoming Role in 'Love Is Never Gone'
item.description: Ara Mina, the celebrated Filipino actress, continues to inspire fans by promoting body positivity and self-acceptance. In a recent interview, she openly discussed how she embraces her natural body and...

item.title: Personal Secretary of BJP Leader Suvendu Adhikari Killed in Madhyamgram, West Bengal
item.description: In a tragic incident in Madhyamgram, West Bengal, the personal secretary of BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari was shot dead. The incident has caused shock and concern among local residents and political cir...

item.title: Anti-Defamation League Reports Significant Rise in Antisemitic Incidents in 2025
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League (ADL) has reported that 2025 experienced the third-highest number of antisemitic incidents in the United States. Jonathan Greenblatt, CEO of the ADL, discussed these finding...

item.title: US Army Seeks Lower-Cost Interceptor to Counter Iran's Cheap Drones
item.description: The U.S. Army is actively seeking to develop a new missile interceptor costing less than $250,000 per unit to address a critical asymmetry in modern warfare. This initiative comes as the military face...
